panels.description: Medium shot: Jonathan Harker sits up abruptly in his ornate bed, disheveled and disoriented, his eyes wide with a lingering unease. Faint sunlight streams through a tall, narrow castle window, casting long, unsettling shadows across the opulent yet eerie room. He clutches his chest, looking around his lavish surroundings with a mix of relief and lingering dread from his dreams.

bubbles.text: I must watch for proof. Of one thing I am glad: if it was that the Count carried me here and undressed me, he must have been hurried in his task, for my pockets are intact.

panels.description: Medium shot: Jonathan Harker, dressed and alert, cautiously approaches a heavy, ornate wooden door in the castle corridor. He reaches out to gingerly touch the door, which is slightly ajar, revealing a broken and splintered lock mechanism on the inside. The corridor is dimly lit, emphasizing the ominous discovery. His expression is a tense mix of suspicion and growing dread.

Medium close-up: Jonathan Harker's face, bathed in the cool light filtering from a distant window. His jaw is clenched, and his eyes are narrowed in deep thought and grim realization, as if recounting a disturbing conversation. The background is a blurry detail of the intricate, ancient castle interior, focusing all attention on his troubled expression and the weight of his thoughts. Lighting is stark, highlighting his drawn features.

bubbles.text: I am surely in the toils. Last night the Count asked me in the suavest tones to write three letters, one saying that my work here was nearly done, and that I should start for home within a few days, another that I was starting on the next morning from the time of the letter, and the third that I had left the castle and arrived at Bistritz.

panels.description: Extreme close-up: Jonathan Harker's eyes, wide with sheer terror and dawning horror, reflecting a harsh, unnatural light source. Beads of sweat glisten on his brow. The pupils are dilated, conveying overwhelming fear. The background is an abstract, dark blur, emphasizing his complete absorption in the horrifying weight of his impossible situation. A dynamic angle and heavy shadows enhance the high intensity of the moment.

Medium shot: Jonathan Harker sits hunched at a large, heavy wooden desk in his room, quill in hand, a half-written letter before him. His posture is slightly slumped with resignation, but his eyes show a flicker of cunning as he ponders his next move, remembering the Count's words about countermanding letters. A faint, cold light filters in from outside, highlighting his isolation and the burden of his strategic thinking.

bubbles.text: He gives me a chance to prolong my stay. But my time is so limited... I must find a way to send word home.

panels.description: Close-up: Jonathan Harker's face, contorted in a silent scream of utter despair. His eyes are squeezed shut, deep lines of anguish etched around them, possibly with tears forming at the corners. His mouth is open in a silent cry as he grapples with the crushing realization of his entrapment and rapidly dwindling time. Dramatic low-key lighting casts heavy shadows, emphasizing the raw, high intensity of his emotion.

Medium close-up: Jonathan Harker, looking up with a sudden, determined glint in his eyes, a flicker of defiance returning to his expression. A small, resolute smile plays on his lips despite his dire circumstances. The background is slightly brighter, perhaps a glimpse of the outside world through a distant window, symbolizing a sliver of newly found hope. His pose suggests readiness to act, a renewed purpose emerging from despair.

bubbles.text: There *is* a chance. I must take it.
